pi_items.source: Task setup_task.sh specifies OUTPUT_DIR=/home/ga/Documents/sagittal_series, confirmed in task.json metadata
Task.json specifies 'png', export_result.sh verifies PNG files using PNG signature validation (b'\x89PNG\r\n\x1a\n')
Downloaded and extracted CT Cranium dataset from https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip, counted DICOM files: 108 total

DICOM analysis: 512x512 axial slices with 108 slices total. When re-sliced in sagittal plane (perpendicular to original), maximum 512 slices possible (one per pixel in lateral x-direction). Verified by pydicom inspection of actual DICOM dataset showing 512-pixel lateral dimension

Not experimentally verified (would require running InVesalius). Range 450-600 is reasonable given: (1) max theoretical sagittal slices = 512, (2) some slices may be empty/skipped, (3) different export strategies may skip edge slices

Not experimentally verified. Upper bound of 600 exceeds theoretical maximum of 512 sagittal slices, possibly allowing for interpolation or other sampling methods. Consistent with verifier threshold of >200 for sagittal detection

privileged_info_summary: The CT Cranium DICOM dataset (case 0051) is sourced from https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip and contains 108 axial CT slices of 512×512 pixels with 0.4785 mm pixel spacing and 2.0 mm slice thickness. The dataset represents actual medical imaging data (non-synthetic). When exported in the sagittal plane, the maximum expected slice count is 512 (one slice per pixel in the lateral x-direction), with each sagittal slice having dimensions of approximately 512×108 pixels and aspect ratio of 4.74:1. The export output should be PNG files saved to /home/ga/Documents/sagittal_series/.
